primary_concern: Sepsis with septic shock and acute kidney injury
clinical_summary: Patient presents with progressive sepsis markers including leukocytosis (WBC 18.7 K/uL), lactic acidosis (4.2 mmol/L), and acute kidney injury (Creat 1.6 mg/dL, AKI Stage 3). Hypotension (BP 94/60 mmHg), peripheral hypoperfusion, and altered mental status (GCS 12) meet Sepsis-3 criteria. Oliguria, thrombocytopenia (88 K/uL), and worsening hyponatremia (132 mEq/L) suggest multi-organ dysfunction.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Persistent hypotension requiring vasopressors and lactate > 2 mmol/L after fluid resuscitation
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent MAP ≥65 mmHg with lactate >2 mmol/L

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine 8.9 mg/dL (Stage 3 AKI) with oliguria <0.5 mL/kg/hr
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets 88 K/uL (<100 K/uL threshold) indicating consumptive coagulopathy

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Administer 30 mL/kg crystalloid for fluid resuscitation
Start norepinephrine infusion to maintain MAP ≥65 mmHg
Perform urgent source control investigation for urosepsis
Initiate continuous renal replacement therapy (CRRT) for AKI Stage 3
doctor_handoff: Review urine culture results for targeted antimicrobial therapy. Assess for potential surgical source of infection. Monitor lactate clearance and urine output response to fluids. Evaluate need for vasopressin augmentation.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: fever, WBC 12.2 K/uL, mild AKI
Progression to septic shock: lactate 3.8 mmol/L, MAP 60 mmHg, GCS 12
AKI Stage 3 with creatinine 8.9 mg/dL (outlier excluded) and oliguria

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
